chapter_title.ar: باب حَدِّ الْوَجْهِ الَّذِي يُغْسَلُ وَ الذِّرَاعَيْنِ وَ كَيْفَ يُغْسَلُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 18 – Limit of the face which should be washed, and the two arms, and how to wash

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول أو ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/92)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول أو ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/92)

parts.text: عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى

text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَا تَضْرِبُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ بِالْمَاءِ ضَرْباً إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتُمْ وَ لَكِنْ شُنُّوا الْمَاءَ شَنّاً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Abdullah Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from his father, from Ibn Al Mugheira, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘Do not wipe your faces with the water with striking when you perform your Ablution (Wudhu)s, but gently splash the water with a gentle splashing’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from his father from ibn al-Mughirah from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The Messenger of Allah has said, “Do not strike water against your face when taking Wudu’. Allow the water to flow over your face.’”
